channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iran has rejected direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter that U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland prime minister says US will not get Greenland
item.description: The prime minister of Greenland is pushing back against assertions by U.S. President Donald Trump that America will take control of the island territory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran if it doesn't agree to nuclear deal
item.description: “If they don’t make a deal, there will be bombing and it will be bombing the likes of which they have never seen before,” Trump said in a comment aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Myanmar's civil war, which is complicating earthquake relief efforts
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The Southeast Asian nation of Myanmar has been plagued by wars and natural disasters since it became independent from Britain in 1948, when it was still called Burma.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France accuses US diplomats of meddling with a 'diktat' about Trump's DEI policies
item.description: A French minister has accused U.S. diplomats of interfering in the operations of French companies by sending them a letter reportedly telling them that U.S. President Donald Trump’s rollback of diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives could also apply outside of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian attack on hospital, shopping center leaves 2 dead as Ukraine braces for fresh offensive
item.description: A Russian drone attack killed two people in Kharkiv on Sunday amid a fresh offensive ahead of peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials survey damage in Myanmar's earthquake-devastated central areas
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Friday’s deadly earthquake rattled most of Myanmar and Thailand but certain areas sustained the heaviest damage, including flattened buildings and many lost lives. The death toll of the 7.7 magnitude earthquake on Friday rose quickly in Myanmar and was at 1,644 victims by Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Turkey arrests Swedish journalist over alleged terrorist connections to a pro-Kurdish group
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish authorities said Sunday they arrested a Swedish journalist dispatched to cover ongoing nationwide protests on charges of terrorism and insulting the president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US immigration officials look to expand social media data collection
item.description: U.S. immigration officials are asking the public and federal agencies to comment on a proposal to collect social media handles from people applying to benefits such as green cards or citizenship, to comply with an executive order from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s denial of Mexico’s Colorado River request sparks concerns over future water negotiations
item.description: The Trump administration’s unprecedented decision to deny a delivery of water to Mexico is raising alarm among experts, who fear it could jeopardize future cross-border negotiations in an increasingly thirsty region.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, The Hill

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Signal scandal fuels growing distrust from allies
item.description: The Trump administration’s sharing of sensitive military information on Signal has shocked allies, while further eroding trans-Atlantic trust thanks to group chat messages from top U.S. officials expressing contempt toward Europe's “freeloading” on security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts has been announced by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government.
item.dc:creator: David Rising And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar on Friday, causing extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries and prompting officials to warn that the initial death toll — above 140 — was likely to grow in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel launched an attack on the Lebanese capital Beirut on Friday for the first time since a ceasefire ended fighting between Israeli forces and the Hezbollah militant group in November.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel on Friday launched an attack on Lebanon's capital for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war in November.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN chief: World leaders must speak with one voice to prevent a new civil war in South Sudan
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations chief urged regional and international leaders Friday to prevent South Sudan from falling “over the abyss” into another civil war and speak with one voice to support a return to peace.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An abrupt announcement rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected international deep-sea waters for more than 30 years. The Metals Company in Vancouver, Canada said late Thursday that it is seeking permission from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Trump plans for Greenland 'has nothing to do' with Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland has "nothing to do" with Russia as Vice President JD Vance lands to assess security base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knife-wielding man injures multiple people in Amsterdam, including 2 Americans
item.description: Dutch police are searching for a motive after five people, including two Americans, were stabbed in a busy shopping area of Amsterdam on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
